text: Micron Stock Dives After Memory Chip Rivals SK Hynix, Samsung Tumble in KOSPI Selloff - Barron's
fullText: Micron stock is dropping after SK Hynix and Samsung stocks tumbled Tuesday on the KOSPI index as the tech selloff gathers pace.

text: ‘What ceasefire?’: In northern Israel, locals doubt an agreement can end the war with Hezbollah - CNN
fullText: In Israel’s northern-most town, Daniel Dorfman knows his pizza shop will be mostly empty all day, just like it has been for weeks. A few customers dine at two tables in the corner. The rest of the restaurant, much like the town it’s in, is deserted.

text: U.S. issues sweeping Iran oil sanctions waivers, unlocking billions in revenue for Tehran - CNBC
fullText: The U.S. issued a sweeping rollback of sanctions on Iranian oil, offering Tehran an economic lifeline as negotiations toward a peace deal proceed.

text: Sexyy Red Claims $500K In Handbags, Jewelry Stolen During Mom’s Birthday - TMZ
fullText: Sexyy Red and her family were the victims of a burglary in Florida, where half a million dollars in handbags and necklaces were stolen ... at least according to a lawsuit she filed against a security guard.

text: Prime Day is almost here: We found 50+ deals to shop early - Mashable
fullText: Amazon devices, Kindle bundles, headphones, and portable power stations are highlights.

text: A dead duck was seen in the Reflecting Pool. Then two more were found nearby. - The Washington Post
fullText: The two ducks that were recovered from Constitution Gardens will undergo necropsies to determine their cause of death.

text: Reports: Heat acquire Giannis Antetokounmpo in blockbuster deal - NBA.com
fullText: The Heat landed Giannis Antetokounmpo from the Bucks on Monday night in exchange for a massive haul of players and draft picks.

text: Authorities arrest 2 more suspects in planned attack on Trump's UFC show - NPR
fullText: Two more people in Missouri and Washington state have been arrested in connection with the planned attack. Law enforcement officials learned about the possible threat on June 10, four days before the event on the White House's South Lawn.

text: Gunman kills an officer at a Montreal hotel before being shot dead, police say - NPR
fullText: Police say a suspect armed with a long gun opened fire at a Montreal hotel, killing a police officer before officers returned fire, killing him.

text: With Starfall, SpaceX eyes an edge in global cargo delivery from orbit - Ars Technica
fullText: The purpose of Starfall is to support the "transport and delivery of goods through space."

text: New images show what’s behind the tarp at the Kennedy Center - CNN
fullText: New images obtained by CNN show the Kennedy Center signage with President Donald Trump’s name removed, confirming a sworn court declaration from the arts center’s executive director Matt Floca that the president’s name is no longer affixed to the building.

text: U.S.-Iran Updates: Vance says Iran to let international nuclear inspections resume after "good day" of talks - CBS News
fullText: Vance says Iran agreed to let international nuclear inspectors back into the country during a "very, very good" first day of negotiations with the U.S.

text: 2 dead, child injured after gunman opens fire inside California library - KTLA
fullText: Two people are dead and a child was injured after a gunman opened fire inside the Butte County Library in Chico, Calif. Monday evening. The Chico Police Department responded to multiple 911 calls for the sound of gunshots and screaming coming from a library o…

text: Clive Davis, music titan behind Whitney Houston, dies at 94 - USA Today
fullText: Clive Davis, the record mogul and music industry titan who discovered talents including Whitney Houston and Barry Manilow, has died.

text: Who is Andy Burnham, U.K.’s ‘King of the North’ and likely next prime minister? - The Washington Post
fullText: The runaway favorite to succeed Prime Minister Keir Starmer is a longtime mayor equally comfortable in the corridors of Westminster and in working-class northern England.

text: Judge blocks use of federal database to check citizenship, saying it could wrongly purge voters - AP News
fullText: A federal judge has ruled that a revamped federal tool that state election officials have used in their efforts to identify illegally registered noncitizen voters is unlawful and cannot be used. U.S. District Court Judge Sparkle L. Sooknanan on Monday sided w…

text: US watchdog opens probe after Tesla crashes into Texas home, killing woman - Al Jazeera
fullText: Road safety regulator investigates crash involving a Model 3 that had been reportedly operating in self-driving mode.

text: Sources: Wolves to trade Randle to Nets; Claxton off to Bulls - ESPN
fullText: The Timberwolves are sending Julius Randle and the No. 28 pick in the NBA draft to the Nets as part of a three-team trade that also will see Nic Claxton go to the Bulls and Minnesota get the No. 33 pick from Brooklyn, sources told ESPN's Shams Charania.

text: World Cup sees first weather delay, with France-Iraq game in Philadelphia paused for 2 hours - AP News
fullText: Heavy rain and thunderstorms prompted a rare World Cup weather delay on Monday, when the halftime break during a match between France and Iraq lasted 2 hours, 10 minutes. It was the first rain delay of the tournament and the first time in at least several dec…

text: Strength Training Is Linked to Longevity, 30-Year Study Finds - ScienceAlert
fullText: Strength training has long been seen as something you mainly do to build muscle or look good.
